Functions.Name: Set_Add
Functions.Description:
Adds item to set. Output value indicates whether the item was successfully added, meaning an 
	  output of False indicates the item was already in the Set.

Params.Name: TargetSet
Params.Description: The set to add item to

Params.Name: NewItem
Params.Description: The item to add to the set

Return.Description: True if NewItem was added to the set (False indicates an equivalent item was present)

Functions.Name: Set_AddItems
Functions.Description: Adds all elements from an Array to a Set

Params.Name: TargetSet
Params.Description: The set to search for the item

Params.Name: NewItems
Params.Description: The items to add to the set

Functions.Name: Set_Remove
Functions.Description:
Remove item from set. Output value indicates if something was actually removed. False
	  indicates no equivalent item was found.

Params.Name: Item
Params.Description: The item to remove from the set

Return.Description: True if an item was removed (False indicates no equivalent item was present)

Functions.Name: Set_RemoveItems
Functions.Description: Removes all elements in an Array from a set.

Params.Name: Items
Params.Description: The items to remove from the set

Functions.Name: Set_ToArray
Functions.Description: Outputs an Array containing copies of the entries of a Set.

Functions.Name: Set_Clear
Functions.Description: Clear a set, removes all content.

Functions.Name: Set_Length
Functions.Description: Get the number of items in a set.

Params.Name: TargetSet
Params.Description: The set to get the length of

Functions.Name: Set_Contains
Functions.Description: Returns true if the set contains the given item.

Params.Name: TargetSet
Params.Description: The set to search for the item

Return.Description: True if the item was found within the set

Functions.Name: Set_Intersection
Functions.Description:
Assigns Result to the intersection of Set A and Set B. That is, Result will contain
	  all elements that are in both Set A and Set B. To intersect with the empty set use
	  Clear.

Functions.Name: Set_Union
Functions.Description:
Assigns Result to the union of two sets, A and B. That is, Result will contain
	  all elements that are in Set A and in addition all elements in Set B. Note that 
	  a Set is a collection of unique elements, so duplicates will be eliminated.

Functions.Name: Set_Difference
Functions.Description:
Assigns Result to the relative difference of two sets, A and B. That is, Result will 
	  contain  all elements that are in Set A but are not found in Set B. Note that the 
	  difference between two sets  is not commutative. The Set whose elements you wish to 
	  preserve should be the first (top) parameter. Also called the relative complement.

Params.Name: B
Params.Description: Set of elements to remove from set A

Params.Name: Result
Params.Description: Set containing all elements in A that are not found in B

Functions.Name: SetSetPropertyByName
Functions.Description: Not exposed to users. Supports setting a set property on an object by name.
